response.justification: Candidate is a strong full-stack software developer with web, mobile, and some embedded background, but the role requires deep hardware-software co-design expertise for AI accelerators — SoC/IP modeling, GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture, and deep neural network workload optimization. The resume shows no evidence of silicon modeling, accelerator performance analysis, or DNN training/inference optimization, and the educational background (BS CS) without the corresponding 8+ years in the relevant hardware/performance domain makes this a poor match for a Principal Performance Architect position.
strengths: Long tenure (15+ years) across diverse software stacks including C/C++ at National Instruments
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience at Dunnhumby (millions of req/sec)
Some embedded and hardware-adjacent exposure (robotic arms, wave field synthesis bachelor thesis)
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, or silicon architecture experience
No deep learning, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or AI accelerator workload background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack product engineering, not performance/systems architecture at the principal hardware level

key_factors.factor: Missing AI accelerator and SoC modeling expertise
No GPU/SIMD/MIMD or DNN optimization background
General low-latency systems and C/C++ exposure
